description: Vela — Go-native knowledge graph builder. Extracts concepts and dependencies from code and docs via Tree-sitter AST and pluggable LLM, builds a gonum graph, clusters with Leiden (Python subprocess), and exports to JSON/HTML/Obsidian with a Bubbletea TUI.

userStories.title: File detection with .velignore support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Vela to walk a directory, collect files by extension, and respect a .velignore file so that only relevant files are processed.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/detect/detect.go with a Collect(root string, exts []string) ([]string, error) function
Walk the directory tree recursively using filepath.WalkDir
Skip paths matching .velignore patterns (same syntax as .gitignore)
Return only files whose extension matches the provided list
Unit tests in internal/detect/detect_test.go cover: empty dir, single match, velignore exclusion, nested dirs
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Bootstrap go-tree-sitter for Go AST extraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need go-tree-sitter installed and a working Go grammar so that subsequent stories can use it for AST parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add github.com/smacker/go-tree-sitter to go.mod
Add tree-sitter Go grammar (smacker/go-tree-sitter/golang)
Create internal/extract/code.go with a ParseGoFile(path string) (*sitter.Node, []byte, error) function that parses a .go file and returns the root AST node
Smoke test: parse cmd/vela/main.go without error
Unit test in internal/extract/code_test.go: parse a fixture Go file and assert root node kind is 'source_file'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Go AST extraction — functions, structs, interfaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extract named functions, struct definitions, and interface definitions from Go source files so that they become graph nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement ExtractGoNodes(root *sitter.Node, src []byte) []types.Node in internal/extract/code.go
Extract: function declarations (kind=function_declaration), type declarations for struct and interface types
Each extracted Node has: ID (file:name), Label (name), Kind (Function|Struct|Interface), File (relative path)
Unit tests cover: file with 1 function, file with 1 struct, file with 1 interface, mixed file
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Go AST extraction — function call edges
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extract function call relationships from Go files so that graph edges represent actual call dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement ExtractGoEdges(root *sitter.Node, src []byte, file string) []types.Edge in internal/extract/code.go
Walk call_expression nodes; record Caller (enclosing function) → Callee (called function) edges
Each Edge has: From (caller ID), To (callee label), Kind='calls', File
Skip stdlib/external calls where callee cannot be resolved to a local node (store as unresolved label, not error)
Unit tests: file with 2 functions where A calls B, assert edge A→B exists
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Extraction dispatcher — code vs doc routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dispatcher that routes each file to the correct extractor (AST for code, LLM for docs) so that the pipeline is extensible.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/extract/extract.go with ExtractAll(files []string, provider types.LLMProvider) ([]types.Node, []types.Edge, error)
Route .go files to ExtractGoNodes + ExtractGoEdges
Route .md, .txt files to a stub DocExtractor that returns empty slices (LLM provider not wired in Phase 0)
Dispatcher logs skipped files at debug level
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Graph construction with gonum
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to build a gonum directed graph from extracted nodes and edges so that graph algorithms can run on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add gonum.org/v1/gonum to go.mod
Implement internal/graph/build.go with Build(nodes []types.Node, edges []types.Edge) (*graph.Graph, error)
types.Graph wraps gonum simple.DirectedGraph with a NodeIndex map[string]int64 for label lookup
Edges whose To node is not in NodeIndex are added as dangling references (not an error)
Unit tests: 3 nodes + 2 edges, assert node count=3, edge count=2
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: JSON export — graph.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to serialize the graph to graph.json so that downstream tools and phases can consume it.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/export/json.go with WriteJSON(g *types.Graph, outDir string) error
Output path: outDir/graph.json
JSON schema: { nodes: [{id, label, kind, file}], edges: [{from, to, kind, file}], meta: {nodeCount, edgeCount, generatedAt} }
Create outDir if it does not exist
Unit test: build a 2-node graph, call WriteJSON, read back and assert node count matches
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Minimal Bubbletea TUI progress display
userStories.description: As a user running vela extract, I want to see real-time progress (current file, % done, elapsed, ETA) so I know the extraction is running and not stuck.
acceptanceCriteria: Wire internal/tui/progress.go into the extraction pipeline via a channel of types.ExtractionProgress
Progress display shows: current file (truncated to 40 chars), chunks done/total, percentage bar, elapsed, ETA
TUI renders without blocking the extraction goroutine
Falls back to plain log output if stdout is not a TTY (CI-safe)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI — vela extract command (Phase 0 integration)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `vela extract <path>` and get a graph.json in vela-out/ so that the Phase 0 PoC success gate is met.
acceptanceCriteria: Wire detect → extract → graph build → JSON export into cmd/vela/main.go via cobra command 'extract'
Default output dir: vela-out/
Flags: --out (output dir), --no-tui (disable TUI, plain logs)
`vela extract .` on the Vela repo itself produces a valid vela-out/graph.json with at least 5 nodes and 1 edge
Non-zero exit code on fatal errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Input validation and security hardening
userStories.description: As a developer, I need path containment checks and label sanitization so that Vela cannot be abused with path traversal inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/security/security.go with ValidatePath(root, target string) error — reject paths that escape root via ../
Implement SanitizeLabel(label string) string — strip non-printable characters and limit to 256 chars
Apply ValidatePath in detect.Collect before walking
Apply SanitizeLabel to all Node.Label values in ExtractGoNodes
Unit tests: path traversal attempt returns error, valid path returns nil, label with special chars is sanitized
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multi-language AST — Python support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vela to extract functions and classes from Python files using Tree-sitter so that Python repos are supported.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tree-sitter Python grammar (smacker/go-tree-sitter/python)
Implement ExtractPythonNodes and ExtractPythonEdges in internal/extract/code.go (same signature pattern as Go)
Extract: function_definition → Node{Kind:Function}, class_definition → Node{Kind:Struct}
Extract call edges: call nodes within function bodies
Register .py extension in dispatcher
Unit test: parse a fixture Python file with 1 function and 1 class, assert 2 nodes extracted
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multi-language AST — TypeScript/JavaScript support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vela to extract functions, classes, and interfaces from TypeScript and JavaScript files.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tree-sitter TypeScript grammar (smacker/go-tree-sitter/typescript)
Implement ExtractTSNodes and ExtractTSEdges in internal/extract/code.go
Extract: function_declaration, arrow_function (assigned to variable), class_declaration, interface_declaration
Register .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx extensions in dispatcher
Unit test: fixture .ts file with 1 class and 1 interface → 2 nodes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: LLM provider interface and local Ollama implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLMProvider interface wired with a working Ollama implementation so that document extraction can use a local model.
acceptanceCriteria: types.LLMProvider interface: Extract(ctx, prompt string) ([]types.Node, []types.Edge, error)
Implement internal/llm/local.go: OllamaProvider hitting http://localhost:11434/api/generate
Request body includes model name and prompt; response is parsed as JSON matching extraction schema
Timeout from config (default 60s)
Return clear error if Ollama is unreachable (not a panic)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM provider — Anthropic Claude implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Claude provider so that users can use frontier model quality for document extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/llm/anthropic.go: AnthropicProvider using messages API
Read API key from config or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
System prompt instructs extraction of nodes and edges as JSON
Parse response into []types.Node, []types.Edge
Return error (not panic) on API failure or malformed response
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM provider — OpenAI GPT-4o implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an OpenAI-compatible provider for GPT-4o and self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/llm/openai.go: OpenAIProvider using chat completions API
Read API key from config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var
Endpoint configurable (default api.openai.com, overridable for local OpenAI-compatible servers)
Parse response into []types.Node, []types.Edge
Return error (not panic) on API failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document extraction — Markdown and text files via LLM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Vela to send markdown and text files to the configured LLM provider for NER and relationship extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/extract/doc.go with ExtractDoc(path string, provider types.LLMProvider) ([]types.Node, []types.Edge, error)
Read file content, build extraction prompt with few-shot examples for NER + RE
Call provider.Extract and merge returned nodes/edges
Register .md, .txt in dispatcher, routing to ExtractDoc when provider != nil
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document chunking for local LLMs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need large documents split into chunks so that models with limited context windows can process them without truncation.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement ChunkText(text string, maxTokens int) []string in internal/extract/doc.go
Use word-boundary splitting (not mid-word); each chunk fits within maxTokens (estimate: 1 token ≈ 4 chars)
Overlap between chunks: last 10% of previous chunk prepended to next chunk for context continuity
ExtractDoc calls ChunkText when len(content) > maxChunkTokens*4 and merges results
Unit tests: short text returns 1 chunk, long text returns N chunks each within limit
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: PDF extraction via text conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PDF files converted to text and sent to the LLM provider for extraction so that architecture docs in PDF format are supported.
acceptanceCriteria: Add github.com/ledongthuc/pdf to go.mod
Implement ExtractPDF(path string, provider types.LLMProvider) ([]types.Node, []types.Edge, error) in internal/extract/doc.go
Convert PDF pages to plain text using ledongthuc/pdf reader
Pass text through ChunkText then to provider.Extract
Register .pdf in dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SHA256 incremental cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a file-level SHA256 cache so that unchanged files are skipped on re-runs, making repeated extractions fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/cache/cache.go with Load(dir string), Save(dir string), IsCached(path, sha string) bool, Mark(path, sha string)
Cache stored as JSON file at cacheDir/cache.json
Before extracting a file, compute SHA256 of its content; skip if IsCached returns true
After successful extraction, call Mark
Unit tests: cache miss on first call, cache hit on second call with same SHA
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Config file — ~/.vela/config.yaml
userStories.description: As a user, I need a config file at ~/.vela/config.yaml so that LLM provider, model, and extraction settings persist across runs without CLI flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement config loading in internal/config/config.go (new file)
Load ~/.vela/config.yaml using yaml.v3; fall back to defaults if file missing
Config struct covers: llm.provider, llm.model, llm.endpoint, llm.api_key, llm.timeout, llm.max_chunk_tokens, extraction.code_languages, extraction.include_docs, extraction.cache_dir, ui.show_progress
Implement `vela config init` subcommand that writes default config to ~/.vela/config.yaml
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: vela doctor — LLM provider health check
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `vela doctor` to check whether my configured LLM provider is reachable before starting a long extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `vela doctor` cobra command
Ping the configured LLM provider endpoint with a minimal request
Print status per provider: OK (green) or UNREACHABLE (red) with endpoint shown
Exit code 0 if all providers healthy, 1 if any unreachable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Leiden clustering via Python subprocess
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Vela to call a Python subprocess running graspologic to run Leiden community detection on the graph and return community assignments.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/graph/cluster.go with RunLeiden(g *types.Graph) (map[string]int, error)
Serialize graph edges to JSON, pass to a bundled Python script via os/exec
Python script (scripts/leiden.py): reads edges JSON from stdin, runs graspologic Leiden, outputs node→community JSON to stdout
Parse Python stdout into map[string]int (node ID → community ID)
Return clear error if Python or graspologic is not installed (not a panic)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Graph analysis — god nodes and surprise edges
userStories.description: As a developer, I need god node detection (highest-degree nodes) and surprise edge scoring so that GRAPH_REPORT.md highlights the most important architectural insights.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/graph/analyze.go with GodNodes(g *types.Graph, topN int) []types.Node
God nodes = topN nodes by total degree (in + out)
Implement SurpriseEdges(g *types.Graph, topN int) []types.Edge — score = cross-community edges ranked by betweenness centrality proxy (degree product of endpoints in different communities)
Implement SuggestedQuestions(nodes []types.Node, edges []types.Edge) []string — return 4 canned question templates populated with actual god node names
Unit tests: 5-node graph, assert top-1 god node is the highest-degree node
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: GRAPH_REPORT.md generation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a human-readable GRAPH_REPORT.md generated after extraction so I can quickly understand the architecture without opening a graph tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/report/report.go with Generate(g *types.Graph, outDir string) error

Report sections: Summary (node/edge count, community count), God Nodes (top 5 with degree), Communities (list with member count), Surprise Edges (top 5 cross-community connections), Suggested Questions (4 questions)
Write to outDir/GRAPH_REPORT.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: HTML export — interactive vis.js visualization
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open graph.html in a browser and interactively explore the knowledge graph with nodes, edges, and community coloring.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/export/html.go with WriteHTML(g *types.Graph, outDir string) error
Embed vis.js Network from CDN in the generated HTML (single self-contained file)
Nodes colored by community ID; god nodes rendered larger
Edges shown as directed arrows
Output: outDir/graph.html
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Obsidian vault export
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export the graph as an Obsidian vault so I can explore the knowledge graph using Obsidian's graph view.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/export/obsidian.go with WriteObsidian(g *types.Graph, outDir string) error
Create outDir/obsidian/ directory
Each node becomes a .md file named {label}.md
File content: frontmatter (kind, file, community), body lists outgoing edges as [[wikilinks]]
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Full Bubbletea TUI — worker pool and provider health
userStories.description: As a user, I want the TUI to show a live worker pool status and LLM provider health so I can monitor concurrent extraction without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement internal/tui/app.go as a full Bubbletea Model with Init/Update/View
Show: provider name + health status (ready/offline), worker pool activity (N workers, N idle), per-file progress rows (file, %, status), overall progress bar
Worker pool: configurable 1-N goroutines via config.workers (default: 2)
TUI updates via tea.Msg channel from extraction goroutines
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TUI — interactive graph query mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch the TUI to a query mode after extraction completes so I can query the graph interactively without leaving the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: After extraction completes, TUI enters query mode with a text input field
Commands: 'path A B' → shortest path between two node labels, 'explain X' → list all edges involving node X, 'quit' → exit
Results displayed in scrollable list below the input
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI query commands — vela query, vela path, vela explain
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI query commands that read an existing graph.json so I can query the graph from scripts without the TUI.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement cobra commands: 'vela query <question>', 'vela path <from> <to>', 'vela explain <node>'
All commands read from vela-out/graph.json by default; --graph flag overrides path
'vela path A B' prints the shortest path as a list of node labels
'vela explain X' prints all edges where X is from or to
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: watch mode — auto-update graph on file changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `vela extract --watch` to automatically re-run extraction when source files change so my graph stays current.
acceptanceCriteria: Add github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify to go.mod
Implement watch loop in cmd/vela: on file change event, re-run extraction for changed file only (incremental via cache invalidation)
Debounce rapid file saves (300ms window)
Print '[watch] re-extracting: <file>' on each update
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MCP server — vela serve
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `vela serve graph.json` to expose the graph via MCP protocol so that AI assistants and tools can query it programmatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `vela serve <graph-file>` cobra command
Start an HTTP server on port 7700 (configurable via --port)
Expose MCP-compatible endpoints: GET /graph (full graph), GET /node/:id (single node), GET /path?from=A&to=B (shortest path)
Serve graph.json content with appropriate Content-Type: application/json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Neo4j export
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to push the graph to a Neo4j instance via Bolt so that I can use Neo4j's query language and visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Add neo4j-go-driver to go.mod
Implement internal/export/neo4j.go with PushNeo4j(g *types.Graph, boltURL string) error
Create nodes as Neo4j nodes with labels matching types.Node.Kind
Create edges as Neo4j relationships with type matching types.Edge.Kind
Expose via --neo4j-push flag on vela extract command
Return clear error if Neo4j is unreachable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Git hooks — vela hook install
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `vela hook install` to install a post-commit git hook that rebuilds the graph on each commit so the graph is always up to date.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `vela hook install` cobra command
Write .git/hooks/post-commit script that runs `vela extract .` in the repo root
Make the hook file executable (0755)
Print confirmation: 'Hook installed at .git/hooks/post-commit'
Typecheck passes
